title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a coding-agent implementation brief after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Change Scope · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Change Scope without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

content.text: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Change Scope, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a coding-agent implementation brief.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A total rewrite is implied whenever existing code is low quality. This fails the Change Scope contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance (2026) — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Implementation Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.

content.text: For Implementation Evidence, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Implementation Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff. Artifact under review: a coding-agent implementation brief.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Implementation Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Implementation Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
